behaviors.description: Embedding vector dimensionality matches the vector index dimension for a query.
behaviors.justification: Real failures here often come from live embedding model/version changes returning a different vector size than the index expects. A stubbed embedder can be forced to the expected size and hide that failure (MOCK-HIDE). This behavior depends on a real AI/ML call output contract (LLM-DEP rule for model-call path), so Integration is too low. Rejected Workflow: behavior text covers a single model-output contract check, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run API with real embedding provider configured and index dimension fixed (e.g., 1536). Send representative queries and assert produced embedding length equals index dimension before/at DB write/search boundary; fail on mismatch.

behaviors.description: Similarity search returns results sorted by descending cosine similarity.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is usually the real vector DB/search engine contract (metric config, ANN behavior, score ordering, tie handling) plus networked service behavior (REAL-INFRA). In-process mocks/fakes can return perfectly sorted data and miss real ordering defects (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Agentic: behavior text is about DB ranking mechanics, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real vector DB instance with seeded vectors/documents, issue search requests via the API and assert returned similarity scores are monotonic non-increasing and IDs match expected top neighbors for fixed query vectors.

behaviors.description: LLM re-ranker reorders at least some candidates for nuanced-intent queries.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM decision quality for nuanced intent; deterministic stubs cannot validate whether reranking meaningfully changes order (LLM-DEP, deterministic-assertion trap). Integration tests can verify wiring but not this behavior’s core failure mode. Rejected Workflow: only one agentic decision point (reranker) is required for the assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fixed candidate set and nuanced queries where semantic intent should alter ranking; call real reranker and assert at least one query produces an order different from raw similarity ranking, with guardrails to avoid flaky trivial swaps (e.g., require top-3 change).

behaviors.description: Queries with no close matches return an empty set instead of low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: Whether a natural-language query is truly ‘no close match’ depends on live embedding/model-space behavior plus retrieval thresholds; stubs can mask miscalibration and false positives (MOCK-HIDE, LLM-DEP model-call path). Integration alone is insufficient if it replaces model outputs. Rejected Workflow: this behavior can be validated at single retrieval decision boundary without requiring multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Seed DB with domain-specific corpus; send out-of-domain queries through real embedding + retrieval pipeline; assert response is empty when top score is below configured relevance cutoff and does not return filler low-score items.

behaviors.description: Median end-to-end query latency (embed + retrieve + rerank + response) stays under 2 seconds.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end SLO across multiple real agentic stages (embedding model call + LLM rerank) and infrastructure boundaries; single-stage System/Agentic tests miss pipeline interaction effects and tail contributions (MULTI-STEP). Rejected System: behavior text explicitly targets full sequential path to final re-ranked response, not one external dependency in isolation.
behaviors.test_description: Run a production-like benchmark over representative queries against real services, collect end-to-end latency from request receipt to response send, and assert median (p50) < 2.0s over sufficient sample size after warm-up.
